title: Causes of death in the over 70s
subtitle: The estimated annual number of deaths from each cause, among people aged 70 years and older. Estimates come with wide uncertainties especially for countries with poor [vital registration](#dod:crvs).
caption:
Causes of death in the over 70s

The estimated annual number of deaths from each cause, among people aged 70 years and older. Estimates come with wide uncertainties especially for countries with poor vital registration.

This horizontal bar chart ranks causes of death for the global population aged 70 and older in 2023, with values ranging from 12.3 million down to 10,200. Cardiovascular disease occupies the top position with a bar extending to 12.3 million, visually surpassing all other categories. Cancers follow at 5.16 million, and chronic respiratory diseases sit at 3.02 million. The next tier includes Alzheimer’s disease and other dementias at 2.04 million, pneumonia at 1.25 million, diabetes mellitus at 1.10 million, and digestive diseases at 1.02 million. Kidney disease appears at 887,000, followed by COVID-19 at 571,000. A cluster of causes falls between 200,000 and 500,000: diarrheal diseases (474,000), Parkinson’s disease (381,000), and tuberculosis (293,000). Below that, road accidents register 143,000, nutritional deficiencies 126,000, and suicide 122,000. The lower portion of the chart shows progressively shorter bars: drowning (38,900), malaria (33,500), fire (33,000), heat-related deaths (28,000), natural disasters (26,600), HIV/AIDS (25,900), alcohol use disorders (20,700), homicide (15,100), hepatitis (10,400), and drug use disorders (10,200). All bars are rendered in uniform blue, arranged in descending order from top to bottom. The subtitle notes that estimates carry wide uncertainties, particularly for countries lacking robust vital registration systems.
